mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during rate cuts show no statistical uplift versus baseline periods (t-stat < 1.5) or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one trading week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 4.0, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines, with quarterly forward returns showing negative alpha or a decayed Rank IC below 0.01 across multiple expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during late-cycle expansions indicates capacity constraints and fading manufacturing momentum, which compresses profit margins and forces negative S&P 500 adjustments over one month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines in late-cycle phases consistently coincide with positive 20-day equity returns, or the mechanism directional accuracy falls below 45% in regime-filtered backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates surpassing 3.0 percent impose restrictive financing conditions that rapidly discount future earnings, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets exhibit positive 5-day returns in over 60% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0, or the threshold signal shows zero incremental predictive power over a flat baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor absorption, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered months, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect strong household balance sheets and construction sector activity, which propagates through supply chains and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Housing starts growth fails to predict quarterly equity direction, with forward returns showing no correlation (Rank IC < 0.02) or consistent underperformance during construction booms.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production indices exceeding 105 signal robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which immediately boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers negative 5-day returns in over 55% of instances when INDPRO crosses above 105, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for concurrent monetary policy shifts.
